titleStmt.title: Letter from the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin, to the Chief Crown Solicitor in Dublin Castle, 17 March 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A series of letters between the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin and the Chief Crown Solicitor Malchy Kelly (1850-1916), Dublin Castle concerning an injury sustained by Patrick Sheehan (1879-?) while he was working as a casual labourer employed by the Forage Department to handle hay, oats and straw. The series of letters concerns possible compensation as this was an injury sustained at work.

correspAction.persName: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref. No. 92.
Subject Injury to Patrick Sheehan. A.A.O., Ireland,
Royal Hospital,
Dublin. 17th March, 1916 The Chief Crown Solicitor,
Dublin Castle Sir, I have yours of 16/3/16 with reference
to Patrick Sheehan and have instructed the D.P.O.S.
Cork to take action as indicated yours faithfully Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer,
M.R.
